gloss.en: the people who control a country or state

words.example: The government raised taxes last year.

gloss.en: a small unit of weight; one thousandth of a kilogram

words.example: This letter weighs only a few grams.

gloss.en: a small unit of weight; one thousandth of a kilogram

words.example: Add fifty grammes of sugar to the bowl.

gloss.en: the child of your son or daughter

words.example: She has five grandchildren.

gloss.en: an informal word for your grandfather

words.example: My granddad tells funny stories.

gloss.en: the daughter of your son or daughter

words.example: His granddaughter is two years old.

gloss.en: the son of your son or daughter

words.example: Her grandson plays football well.

gloss.en: an informal word for your grandmother

words.example: We visit granny every Sunday.

gloss.en: a common green plant that covers fields and lawns

gloss.en: wanting more food, money, or things than you need

gloss.en: to say hello to or welcome someone

gloss.en: the solid surface of the earth

gloss.en: a person you invite to your home or event

gloss.en: a weapon used for firing bullets

words.example: I have never fired a gun in my life.

gloss.en: an informal word for a man

words.example: That guy over there is my brother.

gloss.en: something you do often, almost without thinking

words.example: You need to change your eating habits.

gloss.en: costing half the usual amount

words.example: The shoes were half-price in the sale.

gloss.en: the space inside the front door of a building

words.example: Her brother was standing in the front hall.

gloss.en: meat from a pig's leg, often eaten cold

gloss.en: a small bag a woman carries her things in

gloss.en: having a physical or mental disability

words.example: The hotel has rooms for handicapped guests.

gloss.en: in a cheerful or pleased way

words.example: Children were playing happily on the beach.

gloss.en: to own, hold, or possess something

words.example: He had a new car and a boat.

gloss.en: a continuous pain in the head

words.example: He had a terrible headache.

gloss.en: a device worn over the ears to listen to sound

gloss.en: the teacher in charge of a school

words.example: The headteacher spoke to all the students.

gloss.en: the organ that pumps blood around the body

gloss.en: the quality or feeling of being hot

gloss.en: weighing a lot; hard to lift or move

gloss.en: the act of helping someone do something

words.example: Thank you for all your help.

gloss.en: useful; willing to help

gloss.en: a person admired for being brave or good

words.example: The firefighter was a real hero.

gloss.en: belonging to her

words.example: This book is hers, not mine.

gloss.en: used for a woman or girl who does an action to her own self

words.example: She hurt herself while running.

gloss.en: used to get attention or show surprise

words.example: Hey, can I ask you something?

gloss.en: to put something where it cannot be seen

gloss.en: a long walk in the countryside

gloss.en: the activity of walking long distances in nature

gloss.en: a raised area of land, smaller than a mountain

gloss.en: used for a man or boy who does an action to his own self

words.example: He introduced himself to the class.

gloss.en: a style of music with rhythm and spoken words

words.example: She loves dancing to hip-hop.

gloss.en: a style of music with rhythm and spoken words

words.example: He listens to hiphop every day.

gloss.en: belonging to a man or boy

words.example: James has sold his car.

gloss.en: a team game played with sticks and a ball

words.example: They like to play hockey after school.

gloss.en: a hollow space or opening in something

gloss.en: the place where you live

words.example: She stayed at home all weekend.

gloss.en: a sweet sticky food made by bees

words.example: I like honey on my toast.

gloss.en: great respect, or something that brings pride

gloss.en: a feeling of wanting something to happen

words.example: Don't lose hope—we'll find her.

gloss.en: used to add a contrasting statement

gloss.en: connected with people, not animals or machines

gloss.en: the number 100

gloss.en: a person who hunts wild animals

gloss.en: not well; suffering from a sickness

gloss.en: in a way that matters a lot

gloss.en: not able to happen or be done

gloss.en: to become or make something better

words.example: The situation has improved a lot.

gloss.en: inside an object, area, or space

gloss.en: not costing much money; cheap

gloss.en: a small creature with six legs and three body parts

gloss.en: in the place of someone or something

gloss.en: an object used for making music

words.example: She can play three musical instruments.

gloss.en: good at learning and thinking clearly

gloss.en: the wish to learn more about something

gloss.en: involving two or more countries

gloss.en: to create or design something new

gloss.en: a thing or idea that has been invented

words.example: The phone was a wonderful invention.

gloss.en: to ask someone to come to an event

words.example: Have you been invited to their party?

gloss.en: used for an animal or thing affected by its own action

gloss.en: a sweet food made from fruit and sugar

gloss.en: a soft sweet food that wobbles

gloss.en: something said to make people laugh

gloss.en: an act of travelling from one place to another

gloss.en: old or useless things you do not want

gloss.en: a child or young person

words.example: Do you have any kids?

gloss.en: to make someone or something die

gloss.en: a person or thing that kills

gloss.en: a unit of weight; one thousand grams

gloss.en: a unit of weight equal to one thousand grams

gloss.en: a unit of distance; one thousand metres

gloss.en: the joint in the middle of the leg

gloss.en: information and skills gained from learning

gloss.en: a device that produces light

gloss.en: pale in colour; not dark
